codeReview.summary: The repair adequately closes the prior failure by making the auditable handoff commit point at a commit that actually includes the claimed doc update, canonical tracking status block, and backend regression proving global single-running-timer behavior across workspaces.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The original worker reported commit `54b030b` as the feature handoff even though that commit did not contain the claimed `docs/product/tracking.md` update or cross-workspace regression; the required step to verify the handoff commit diff was either not followed or not strong enough to catch the mismatch.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: tracking-backend-worker/SKILL.md step 10 requires verifying the handoff commit itself contains the claimed work. `git show --stat 54b030b` shows only `apps/backend/internal/tracking/application/security_regressions_test.go`, while the original handoff JSON claimed doc and regression changes. The fix handoff at mission handoffs/2026-03-25T04-12-49-459Z__timer-core-global-running-contract-handoff-repair__9e861239-4d02-45d1-9f7a-23726151a855.json explicitly corrects this mismatch.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the prior failed feature handoff, the repair handoff, the fix transcript skeleton, and both relevant diffs. The original failed handoff pointed at the wrong commit, but the repair commit `320c451` fixes the gitignore problem and makes the repo contain the auditable `.factory` mission state while the claimed product-doc change and `TestRunningTimerConflictAcrossWorkspaces` regression are present in the codebase, so the original failure is adequately addressed.
